reason: Both correctly advise against full event sourcing and recommend an append-only ledger as the pragmatic middle ground — no factual errors in either. But Answer B is extremely terse: one paragraph, no mention of considering an existing billing provider (Stripe/Metronome/Lago), no discussion of why full ES specifically hurts billing (versioning/re-rating historical invoices, eventual consistency UX issues), and no acknowledgment of the "enthusiasm for the pattern, not the problem" red flag. Answer A hits all of these: it gives concrete architectural guidance (ledger for money-related tables, CRUD for subscriptions/plans, idempotent usage ingestion), explains concretely why full ES creates real pain in a billing context (proration/re-rating dilemma, projection lag causing bad billing UX), explicitly suggests evaluating existing billing platforms before building, and closes with a nod to the team's desire to use ES by scoping it narrowly if needed. This is substantive decision-relevant detail, not padding — each point adds actionable value. B's brevity comes at the cost of missing several of the key points a competent answer should cover.
